title: Fast Company roundup: Oracle, Capital One, Morgan Stanley, Block, and eBay layoffs
excerpt: Fast Company reviewed a brutal week of tech and finance layoffs and explicitly noted that not all of the layoffs were because of AI.
notes: Reviewed 2026-06-03. Oracle, Block, and eBay are already represented in verified entries and now include Fast Company as a source. Capital One is not AI-related per Fast Company. Morgan Stanley 2026 has no clean reputable AI-causal source in the Fast Company/WSJ/AP/Reuters framing; keep as review-only unless stronger original reporting is found.

title: Capital One lays off more than 1,100 former Discover workers
excerpt: Capital One layoffs followed its Discover acquisition and affected former Discover roles in Riverwoods, Illinois.
notes: Rejected for public AI-layoffs dataset on 2026-06-03. Fast Company explicitly says these layoffs have nothing to do with AI and ties them to Capital One integrating Discover.

title: Morgan Stanley 2026 layoff round affecting about 2,500 employees
excerpt: Morgan Stanley laid off roughly 2,500 employees, about 3% of its workforce, across business lines and support functions.
notes: Do not publish yet. Fast Company and WSJ framing ties the cuts to shifting business/location priorities, job performance, and support functions, not a direct AI rationale. Search found secondary commentary claiming a NY Post AI angle, but a stronger original/reputable AI-causal source is needed before adding a separate 2026 public entry. Existing public Morgan Stanley entry is the separate 2025-03-19 event.

title: Uber cuts 23% of People and Places division roles
excerpt: Uber cut 23% of positions in its People and Places division, less than 1% of its global workforce, as part of an organizational restructuring under president Jill Hazelbaker.
notes: Rejected for public AI-layoffs dataset on 2026-06-04. The story has AI context in the URL/news cycle, but accessible CNBC/Bloomberg follow-up reporting says a company spokesperson stated the cuts were unrelated to artificial intelligence initiatives. Treat as a false-positive lead unless a stronger primary or reputable source later ties the reduction to AI.
